message:
Add comprehensive conformance tests for q (quick capture) command

Introduces a complete test suite for the `br q` / `bd q` quick capture
command, validating that the Rust implementation produces equivalent
behavior to the Go reference implementation.

Test coverage includes:

Basic functionality:
- conformance_q_basic: Verifies quick capture outputs just an ID (no JSON)
- conformance_q_creates_issue: Confirms the captured issue can be retrieved
- conformance_q_multiple_creates_different_ids: Each capture gets unique ID

Flag combinations:
- conformance_q_with_priority: Tests -p/--priority flag
- conformance_q_with_type: Tests -t/--type flag
- conformance_q_with_labels: Tests -l/--label flag(s)
- conformance_q_all_flags: Tests combining priority, type, and labels

Edge cases:
- conformance_q_empty_title_fails: Validates error on empty title
- conformance_q_special_characters_title: Unicode and punctuation handling
- conformance_q_very_long_title: Tests title length limits
- conformance_q_multiword_title: Ensures multi-word titles work without quotes
- conformance_q_numeric_title: Titles that look like numbers
- conformance_q_whitespace_title: Titles with leading/trailing/internal spaces

Output format validation:
- conformance_q_output_format_plain: Ensures plain text ID output (not JSON)
- conformance_q_id_format: Validates ID matches expected bd-XXXX pattern
- conformance_q_no_extra_output: Confirms no spurious stdout/stderr

Integration:
- conformance_q_issue_in_list: Created issues appear in `list` output
- conformance_q_issue_searchable: Created issues are searchable

All tests use the ConformanceWorkspace harness to run equivalent commands
on both br (Rust) and bd (Go), comparing outputs for consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
